author: @giovannip.1433
text: Often small front wheels make maneuvering difficult as many roads and footpaths are uneven resulting in getting stuck or loosing control. Unfortunately bigger front wheels result in increased wheelbase - making it less practical in tight environments or a smaller relative wheelbase which may make the vehicle more 'tippy'.

author: @captainprototype187
text: I found ani nimh 28v 2.2 apm pack in a vacuum cleaner. One resistor oft he charging circuit seems to be bad.

text: Awesome guys 😄

author: @stevetobias4890

author: @grantwinn54
text: There you go a cheap diy wheelchair with steering. Hey may I suggest two things maybe widen the front end to make it more stable and add some armrests.

text: Lipo4 is what I would use for that, but price would go up. Bms are not cheap. Good would, Luke look like it had a bit of fun too

replies.author: @twestgard2
replies.text: I bm once a day or more and I can’t remember ever paying a fee.  Hmm🤔

author: @hempev
text: Entertaining and educational as usual.

author: @martinbonner8626
text: Add some magnets to the wheels and have a recharging system for the batteries

replies.author: @paulbrouyere1735
replies.text: I’m sorry but I don’t think Robert invented a perpetuum mobile with this project.

author: @Arachnoid_of_the_underverse
text: Looks excellent for a cheap ride. Just thinking an alternative, if you moved the lever acting against the wheels to the outside and placed the steering rod/tiller to one side it would then have correct steering orientation.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: could do - but don't forget this tiller folds down for getting in and out

author: @banana1618
text: always a joy to watch what you both are up to - and usually it is something unusual and pretty innovative: wtg!

author: @simonsimpson4695
text: Fantastic! I had in mind an office chair for the seat. Obviously fixing it so it doesn't revolve unexpectedly! Having a spare car seat is great and more comfortable for travel. The information on the steering etc  is so valuable and something I had no idea about. Clear explanation and easy to follow. I'm so looking forward to this build!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I thought about an office chair too - but to be honest if you are going to spend a bit of time in it the car seat is far more comfortable and formed to body contours better - still an office chair would be good as the basis for a folding version I was thinking - what do you think?

replies.author: @simonsimpson4695
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Yes, absolutely.  It would be very useful to have it folding. With what you described for the mechanism (motor and drive train) being a fairly simple affair and low power etc it's should be relatively light minus the battery.  I spent a considerable number of years sitting in the very most basic of office swivel chairs, the kind that must be ten a penny from used office supplies/ car boots.  They are strong and of course already have a subframe, if that's the right terminology.  I'm looking at cycle wheels and using a single driven rear wheel of a small bicycle. I'll need fabricate a frame to mount the motor and fix the seat etc.  The trike idea is an elegant solution but might be beyond my ability.  Thanks for mentioning the other videos.  I'd like to somehow mount the steering to one side still using a mechanical set up. Not sure if it's possible.

author: @McRootbeer
text: Idea for the next iteration: a no weld folding paper wheelchair.  With stair climbing wheels 😁.

author: @angelusmendez5084
text: Awesome! 👏

author: @AM-us7cz
text: You and the things that create are bloody Brilliant Rob 👏 thanks mate.

author: @docink6175
text: having ridden several mobility scooters over varying terrain I would suggest pneumatic tires on the front for the next incarnation, even my fairly expensive scooter rides terribly over board walks... personally Id just attach the car seat to the trike frame

replies.author: @lisataylor3085
replies.text: agreed, those little wheels might cause it to tip.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - you are going to laugh when you see the next vid lol

author: @karlmyers6518
text: It doesn't look as fun and scary but 100% more practical.  Well done ✔

author: @PsoriasisChannel
text: We call them shopping carts here in USA 🇺🇸 Lol 😂.    Love ❤️ your work Robert Murray-Smith!

author: @Zodliness
text: Considering I paid £2500 + accessories for my power wheelchair, making one in this sort of way, would have left me with some money to spend, after the build.

replies.author: @Zodliness
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering most power chair will accommodate a passenger with a max weight of 18st, that's me FECKED for anything that fits my needs. They're not cheap and many mobility shops love to markup the cost.
